primary_reason: The study uses a multicenter pre-shortage versus shortage comparison to benchmark changes and institutional variation in antibiotic prescribing for acute respiratory infections.
This comment discusses whether endophthalmitis benchmarks can benefit patients but supplies no substantive methodological content.
The paper prescribes laboratory procedures for gastrointestinal epithelium and cancer organoids without an infectious-disease application or concrete epidemiologic transfer route.
The study applies an agent-based HIV model informed by surveillance and cohort data to forecast comorbidity patterns across demographic and acquisition-risk subgroups.
The authors develop an open-source synthetic-data platform and demonstrate reusable sepsis and HIV datasets for health-data-science training and analysis.
This retraction notice only identifies a withdrawn article and adds no methodological, diagnostic, correction-analysis, or reproducibility content.
The study applies an agent-based model to compare how activity patterns, group sizes, and vaccination affect SARS-CoV-2 transmission in jails.
The authors construct an agent-based model of CVB4 antibody enhancement and neutralization and confront its mechanistic predictions with in-vitro experiments.
The study develops and externally validates a Monte Carlo model for forecasting COVID-19 ICU occupancy and associated nursing complexity.
The study builds and calibrates an agent-based B-cell model to explain immune memory and impaired responsiveness under sepsis- and COVID-associated inflammatory conditions.
Using synthetic MRSA transmission trajectories, the study evaluates parameter identifiability in an agent-based model coupled to an Ensemble Adjustment Kalman Filter and proposes ways to improve it.
The study validates AlphaMissense against cystic-fibrosis variants using molecular, clinical, and Pseudomonas infection metrics, revealing metric-dependent predictive limitations.
This family case report uses sequencing and molecular docking to characterize monogenic inflammatory bowel disease without an infectious-disease outcome or concrete transfer route.

The study compares benchmark lipid-nanoparticle formulations across RNA cargo sizes and identifies a consequential discrepancy between conventional and input-based encapsulation measurements that can inform mRNA-vaccine formulation.
The study uses molecular-dynamics simulations to determine how βGln114 mutations alter an antimicrobial target required by multiple infectious pathogens.
This scoping review categorizes 154 HIV agent-based-model applications and assesses their purposes, populations, reporting of the ODD protocol, and acknowledgment of limitations.
The authors develop an interaction-driven airborne-pathogen model and use real and history-preserving synthetic networks to compare temporal, spatial, and spatiotemporal distancing policies.
The study applies a new agent-based plant-virus model integrating mixed infection, within-host competition, transmission distance, environmental survival, and control interventions.
The authors develop a contrastive-learning and clustering strategy for weakly annotated dermatology images and demonstrate transfer to monkeypox detection with limited additional training data.
The study uses a dynamic epidemic model to estimate COVID-19 deaths under counterfactual obesity distributions and earlier food-labeling or taxation policies in Mexico.
The study standardizes reproducible mouse challenge assays against RTS,S/AS01 and evaluates their range and predictive capability for comparing next-generation malaria vaccines.
The study integrates a fitted antibody-efficacy model with a malaria transmission model to project vaccine impact and cost-effectiveness across transmission settings.
The agent-based intervention analysis concerns opioid overdose and provides no concrete route to infectious-disease transmission, surveillance, or control.
The authors develop a modular agent-based framework for pathogen and antimicrobial-resistance transmission through realistic poultry production and distribution networks.
The study develops severity-adjusted hospital benchmarking based on mixed-effects observed-to-expected ratios for postoperative infection and antibiotic utilization.
The authors construct an updateable nonredundant antibody-antigen docking benchmark and compare several prediction methods to support vaccine and therapeutic algorithm development.
The paper documents the design and implementation of a stochastic agent-based COVID-19 model representing population attributes, tract-level contacts, behavior, interventions, and variants.
The study simulates adaptive platform and stand-alone COVID-19 trials to compare development time, sample size, and statistical operating characteristics under varied design assumptions.
The study simulates mass gatherings under varying prevalence, size, and age structures to quantify SARS-CoV-2 rapid-test yield, predictive values, and cost per detected case.
The authors develop a mechanistic model of age-dependent human thymocyte dynamics that can support predictive immune-response and infectious-disease drug-development simulations.
The study benchmarks human-genome structural-variant tools without a pathogen, infectious outcome, or specific infectious-surveillance application.
The study powers an agent-based nursing-home outbreak model with sensor-derived contact data to compare screening, isolation, contact restrictions, presenteeism, and staffing schedules.
The prospective study evaluates CRISPR-BP34 against culture and independent PCR adjudication, quantifying diagnostic speed, sensitivity, and specificity for melioidosis specimens.
The study validates an improved SEIR model and simulates infections, undetected cases, and nucleic-acid-testing resource needs under six control scenarios.
The study calibrates an agent-based epidemic and opinion-dynamics model with survey and COVID-19 case data from 15 countries to assess how public opinion affects transmission.
The authors develop and jointly calibrate a flexible agent-based representation of traditional and spheroid in-vitro tuberculosis models for integrating host-pathogen experiments.
The bootstrap analysis examines disability-employment inequities during the COVID-19 period but has no infectious outcome or concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
The study uses a networked SEIR simulation with fixed testing capacity and noncompliance to compare symptomatic, random individual, and pooled COVID-19 testing policies.
The study combines experiments with a mechanistic within-mosquito dengue model to quantify dose-dependent midgut escape and extrinsic incubation dynamics.
The study applies an agent-based biofilm model to optimize periodic antibiotic dosing across differing persister-cell dynamics.

The paper develops a scalable operational pipeline combining automated computation, disaggregated data, national contact-network digital twins, and agent-based epidemic simulation for real-time response.
The multicenter study uses surgery-stratified survival analysis and adjusted complication benchmarks that include postoperative infection outcomes.
The study systematically compares three biophysical affinity methods across benchmark G4 ligands and proposes a reference compound for rapid screening applicable to antiviral G4-drug development.
The study applies a geospatial agent-based model to estimate how disaster timing and duration alter COVID-19 transmission and mortality.
The study uses an ICU resistance model and its synthetic data to estimate antimicrobial de-escalation effects and the trial size and duration needed to detect them.
The study compares processed implant materials using standardized bacterial-growth assays and in-vivo infection observations alongside molecular simulation and biocompatibility measurements.
The study links ingestible-sensor adherence measurements to dried-blood-spot drug-metabolite concentrations to establish quantitative adherence benchmarks for HIV therapy.
The authors develop and validate a reusable multimodal embedding and transfer-learning ensemble framework for antiviral drug repurposing, including a monkeypox case study.
The study combines schistosomiasis egg-count analysis with transmission simulation to compare diagnostic sensitivity, stopping thresholds, rebound risk, health impact, and survey costs.
The study uses computational design, docking, molecular-dynamics simulation, and MM-GBSA analysis to identify a candidate dengue RNA-helicase inhibitor.
The study measures unbilled provider time per OPAT course to establish transferable workload and payment benchmarks for infectious-disease services.
The study develops and compares machine-learning surrogate models for detecting tipping points and quantifying rare-event uncertainty in agent-based simulators, including a network epidemic model.
The study uses a two-subpopulation SEIIS simulation to examine how hesitancy, rollout speed, variants, and nonpharmaceutical interventions constrain a hypothetical pan-coronavirus vaccine.
The study applies virtual screening, molecular docking, and molecular-dynamics simulation to assess phytochemicals against targets from four human viral pathogens.
The study uses a calibrated agent-based COVID-19 transmission model to compare mass, risk-targeted, age-targeted, and ring-vaccination strategies under varying supplies and epidemic waves.
The study combines molecular docking, molecular-dynamics simulation, and MM-GBSA analysis to evaluate marine phlorotannins as SARS-CoV-2 main-protease inhibitors.
The multicenter study benchmarks twelve short- and long-read viral metagenomic protocols with a mock reference panel and derives protocol-specific positivity thresholds, sensitivity, and specificity.
The study develops a population pharmacokinetic model and uses Monte Carlo simulation to derive weight-sensitive intravenous posaconazole regimens for invasive aspergillosis.
The study uses extensive simulations to evaluate how treatment-selection rules and stopping boundaries affect sample size, power, and familywise error in multi-arm multi-stage trials.
The study calibrates an agent-based model to aged-care outbreaks and simulates how community prevalence, booster timing, incursions, and nonpharmaceutical interventions affect COVID-19 outcomes.

The study develops an implantable intracranial sensor for physiological monitoring, while mentioning infection only as a disadvantage of wired devices and providing no concrete infectious-disease transfer route.

The article uses the authors' Florida COVID-19 modeling experience to explain when simple versus fine-scale agent-based models are appropriate and how real-time evidence and decision-support demands affect model use.

The protocol combines directly observed dosing, validated drug measurements across biological matrices, pharmacokinetic modeling, and multiple imputation to estimate PrEP adherence–concentration–efficacy thresholds in African women.
The study develops an agent-based SARS-CoV-2 model of an abstract city and simulates how event characteristics, masking, and lockdown strategies alter population incidence and individual risk.
The review compares cellular, animal, and xenograft models of neuropsychiatric and brain diseases without identifying a concrete route to infectious-disease epidemiology.
The study constructs and analyzes a mechanistic HIV/AIDS model, fits it to case data, assesses equilibrium stability, and performs reproduction-number sensitivity analysis.
The study reports standard antibacterial and antibiofilm testing of a vancomycin-loaded nanocomposite but does not contribute a transferable epidemiologic design, model, or measurement method.
The study combines viable-spore PMA-qPCR, size-resolved aerosol sampling, pathogen isolation, and experimental infection thresholds to characterize airborne transmission of a plant fungal pathogen.

The article introduces an open-source multiscale agent-based HPV simulator with country-specific demography, sexual networks, multiple genotypes, disease progression, intervention modules, tests, and documentation.
The study benchmarks 132 assembly and polishing pipelines on closely related outbreak isolates and derives best practices for producing bacterial genomes accurate enough for source tracking.
The article defines eight efficacy, resistance, safety, formulation, and affordability benchmarks for novel antiretroviral regimens and applies them to two development programs.

The study calibrates agent-based simulations to experimental tuberculosis granuloma models and uses mechanism-removal scenarios to explain how spatial structure and cell movement affect bacterial control.
The study uses Monte Carlo PK/PD simulation to compare ceftazidime-avibactam doses and infusion durations against multiple exposure targets and regional MIC distributions.
The study uses virtual screening, docking, ADME assessment, and molecular-dynamics simulation to identify a candidate inhibitor of a uropathogenic Escherichia coli flagellar protein.
The study examines how demographic, network, behavioral, and geographic heterogeneity affect the complexity, calibration, computation, and outputs of a national agent-based epidemic scenario model.

The study develops the network measure of inversity, proves performance properties for two privacy-sensitive intervention strategies, and evaluates them through generated and real networks with epidemic simulations.

The article integrates an agent-based immune-system simulator with physiologically based kinetic models to predict PFAS effects on vaccine antibody responses, providing a concrete transfer route through vaccination-outcome modeling.
The study uses an agent-based wildlife model to simulate how acute hemorrhagic-disease outbreaks alter chronic-wasting-disease transmission and deer population dynamics.

The study develops and validates an agent-based method for reconstructing three-dimensional immune-cell trajectories from two-dimensional measurements, which can transfer to studies of immune migration during host–pathogen interactions.

The study presents an agent-based proof-of-concept model linking poultry production, antimicrobial use, infection control, producer behavior, and economic incentives for AMR policy development in Senegal.
The study pools pharmacokinetic data, develops a nonlinear mixed-effects model, and simulates gestational-age dosing bands for lamivudine in preterm infants exposed to HIV.
The study combines real-world intensive-care records with simulation to compare ten score-based and non-score-based tertiary-triage policies in a COVID-19 resource-crisis setting.
The study develops and validates a multiplex stool PCR for three gastrointestinal parasites by benchmarking its agreement and detection performance against microscopy and simplex PCR.
The single-group trial evaluates a therapy for alloantibody-mediated fetal anemia, and its infection safety observations do not establish a concrete infectious-disease methodological transfer route.
The study combines Bayesian networks and Monte Carlo simulation to estimate residual HIV, HBV, and HCV transfusion risks under alternative pathogen-reduction and donor-deferral policies.
The study builds district-level dynamic agent-based models to estimate how rotavirus vaccination changes infections, deaths, inequities, and inappropriate antibiotic use across India.

The study uses causal diagrams and simulations to diagnose collider bias, behavioral confounding, and outcome misclassification caused by including asymptomatic people in test-negative vaccine-effectiveness studies.
The matched-cohort study reports hazard-ratio comparisons of COVID-19 reinfection, breakthrough infection, and mortality without contributing a reusable design, model, or measurement method.

The study uses causal diagrams, simulated reporting scenarios, and Bayesian posterior predictive analysis to identify how reaction severity and healthcare-seeking behavior generate missed or false vaccine-safety signals.
The study uses a stochastic multiscale transmission model with superinfection and hypnozoite dynamics to estimate Plasmodium vivax elimination probabilities under repeated mass drug administration.

The study combines risk-adjusted hospital-specific survival models, scan statistics, temporal adjustment, and randomized-time comparisons to assess clustering of complications including surgical-site infections.
The population-based birth cohort uses infant RSV surveillance to measure infection, symptoms, lower-respiratory disease, and hospitalization as benchmarks for evaluating new preventive products.
The study uses docking and molecular-dynamics simulation to investigate a diabetic kidney disease biomarker without an infectious outcome or a concrete infectious-disease transfer route.

The title indicates discussion of synthetic data in infectious-disease research, but the absent abstract leaves insufficient information to determine whether the article provides substantive methodology or only commentary.

The validation study repeatedly generates privacy-preserving synthetic health records and compares COVID-19 vaccine-effectiveness estimates with original-data results using multiple agreement and stability metrics.
The study applies molecular docking and molecular-dynamics simulation to evaluate plant-derived cardenolides as candidate inhibitors of HIV-1 protease.

The study benchmarks deep-learning and traditional variant callers across sequencing models, read types, bacterial species, and depths using a validated truth set relevant to outbreak tracking and AMR detection.

The study develops and tests a dairy-industry detergent-disinfectant using standard antimicrobial, toxicity, and cleaning outcomes without contributing a transferable infectious-disease epidemiology method.
The study combines a hereditary neuropathy case series with molecular simulation of a SEPTIN9 mutation and provides no concrete infectious-disease transfer route.

The study develops an experimentally supported mechanistic model linking IgG subclass oligomerization to complement recruitment and activation, with a concrete transfer route to antibody therapies targeting infectious agents.

The study combines population pharmacokinetic models, tissue penetration assumptions, and simulated target-attainment analyses to evaluate monitoring strategies for antibiotic combinations at infection sites.
The study comparatively evaluates three nucleic-acid vaccine platforms against a benchmark vaccine using immune-response and protection measurements in a Hantaan virus mouse model.
The study uses interviews, a professional focus group, and comparative care-cascade analysis to evaluate the acceptability, replicability, and effectiveness of an HCV micro-elimination intervention.

The review substantively examines generative agent-based modeling with large language models, including applications to epidemic propagation and methodological problems involving prompt sensitivity, bias, and standardization.
The study integrates interaction screening, in vitro validation, structural modeling, molecular docking, and simulation to investigate a mechanistic protein interaction in Entamoeba histolytica.
The record releases a cleaned and normalized patient-level dengue hematology dataset intended to support reproducible severity prediction and cross-population benchmarking.
The agent-based study uses census-derived populations to simulate how vaccination coverage and household composition contribute to racial differences in influenza incidence.

The computational study combines virtual screening, molecular docking, molecular-dynamics simulation, and pharmacokinetic prediction to identify candidate inhibitors of a Mycobacterium tuberculosis metabolic target.
The study applies and prospectively validates an agent-based model for national and regional forecasts of SARS-CoV-2 cases, hospitalizations, intensive-care admissions, and deaths in Poland.
The study uses a race-stratified agent-based influenza transmission model to compare idealized interventions for reducing inequities in symptomatic infection and hospitalization.
The study uses cohort-informed Monte Carlo simulations to compare how single and combination enteric vaccines could reduce antibiotic use and bystander-pathogen exposure across five settings.
The study benchmarks molecular dynamics, protein-protein docking, and AlphaFold complex prediction against control SARS-CoV-2 variants and explains each method's errors and practical tradeoffs.

The study descriptively profiles sausage quality and microbial contamination using standard multivariate analyses without identifying a transferable infectious-disease design or measurement contribution.

The study constructs optimistic and pessimistic composite performance indicators to benchmark hospital resilience across COVID-19 waves, providing a transferable design for evaluating health-system preparedness during infectious crises.

The analysis combines notification, cohort, administrative, and life-table data to apply population-specific incidence-prevalence ratio benchmarks where reliable HIV population denominators are unavailable.

The systematic review compares the epidemiological and computational characteristics of tuberculosis agent-based models and identifies limitations in spatial structure, parameter tuning, reporting, and code availability.
The synthetic-data study evaluates simulation-based calibration for MCMC and approximate Bayesian computation and shows how calibration errors can remain hidden by conventional model validation.
The study observes simulated pediatric resuscitations and measures the time-neutral feasibility of performing WHO-defined hand-disinfection actions during emergency care.
The scoping review develops a five-level taxonomy of agent-based blood-borne-virus models and assesses gaps in represented populations, transmission mechanisms, interventions, and social factors.

The study combines an evidence-derived governance framework, expert review, and structured gap analysis to assess dialysis care, including infection-prevention and control procedures that can transfer to infectious-risk service assessments.
The study develops a sustained-release collagen hydrogel and evaluates preventive and therapeutic delivery of antischistosomal drugs using infection, worm, egg, and liver outcomes in mice.
The study benchmarks a scalable liposomal adjuvant against established formulations using innate-immune assays and comparative CMV vaccine responses in mice and nonhuman primates.
The study models volatility in a healthcare stock index around the onset of COVID-19 without an infection outcome or a concrete route to infectious-disease epidemiologic methodology.
The study uses a pediatric breathing simulator to compare nebulizer technologies and quantify delivered tobramycin doses across child and tracheostomy-tube configurations.
This published erratum only identifies the corrected Ebola simulation article and supplies no substantive correction or methodological content of its own.
The study builds a sociodemographic and mobility-informed population network and simulates COVID-19 transmission and intervention timing in Catalonia over two years.
The study extends an SEIR structure into a heterogeneous agent-based model and compares vaccination-priority strategies across vaccine-availability and epidemic-spread scenarios.
The record is a comment on generative agent-based epidemic modeling and provides no abstract or other supplied evidence of substantive methodological content.
The study uses an agent-based health-care-seeking model to test whether EHR capture mechanisms could generate observed geographic disparities in community-associated MRSA prevalence.
The study develops an open-source, large-scale agent-based framework with an India-specific synthetic population and demonstrates it through COVID-19 intervention and variant scenarios.
The study assays human protein-domain variants for genetic-disease interpretation and computational benchmarking without an infectious outcome or a stated infectious-disease transfer route.
The study estimates the sensitivity, specificity, predictive values, and missed-lesion frequency of high-risk HPV testing against cytology and biopsy to evaluate cervical-screening strategies.
The study couples agent-based modeling with ordinary differential equations to examine how interspecies interactions and drug properties alter antibiotic pharmacodynamics in polymicrobial infections.
The study combines antifungal assays, quantitative structure-activity modeling, enzyme inhibition, homology modeling, and molecular docking to evaluate inhibitors of plant-pathogenic fungi.
The study uses synthetic data to model inflammatory responses in osteoarthritis without an infectious outcome or a concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
The study evaluates whether antimicrobial-use-to-resistance ratios provide more interpretable stewardship benchmarks than antimicrobial-use measures alone across two hospitals.
The study develops and calibrates a stochastic feedlot agent-based model to compare antimicrobial selection and contagious transmission explanations for resistance emergence.
The simulation study compares disease-risk-score and multivariable confounding adjustments for multinomial vaccination exposures in test-negative COVID-19 effectiveness studies.
The study develops a mechanistic model combining mutational, antibody-pharmacokinetic, and genomic-surveillance data to explain and forecast regional SARS-CoV-2 variant dynamics.
The study creates a labeled fluorescence microscopy benchmark and develops a lightweight deep-learning detector for cellular and pathogenic indicators in vaginal-discharge specimens.

The retrospective chart review reports ventilator-associated pneumonia rates and resource use against published benchmarks without developing a reusable adjustment, surveillance, or evaluation method.
The study uses an experimentally informed agent-based model to examine how macrophage and CD4-positive T-cell interaction timing affects early tuberculosis control and granuloma formation.
The record establishes a public repository of curated foodborne-pathogen datasets and customizable modeling scripts to support reproducible machine-learning development and validation.
The Monte Carlo study estimates operational cost differences between microneedle patches and conventional vaccine delivery across diseases, vial formats, and routine or campaign use cases.

The study develops an integrated agent-based framework that reconciles disease, mobility, household, and wastewater processes across spatial and temporal resolutions for communicable-disease surveillance.
The study uses a census-based agent model to quantify direct and indirect effects of increased childhood influenza vaccination on cases and hospitalizations across age groups.
The study develops a multilevel risk-adjusted smoothed observed-to-expected ratio for benchmarking broad-spectrum antibiotic use while limiting distortion from hospital variation and extreme values.

The computational study combines natural-compound screening, docking, microsecond molecular-dynamics simulations, and post-simulation interaction estimates to identify candidate inhibitors of a Pseudomonas resistance enzyme.
The study characterizes cardiac-channel variants and benchmarks genetic pathogenicity predictors without an infectious outcome or a stated route to infectious-disease methods.
The study benchmarks four structure-based virtual-screening tools for wild-type and Omicron SARS-CoV-2 proteases and validates a selected protocol with molecular-dynamics calculations.
